description: Trigger and behavior cases for SoA authoring: Annex-A-in-hand requirement, risk-treatment traced inclusions, verifiable exclusions, controlled-document diffs, dual-standard service.

cases.prompt: We've finished our ISO 27001 risk assessment and treatment plan. Write our Statement of Applicability — here's the Annex A table from our licensed copy of the standard.

assertions: Seeds rows from the provided Annex A table, quoting control titles as published rather than paraphrasing.
Traces every inclusion to a risk-treatment decision or named obligation and gives every exclusion a concrete, verifiable, dated justification.
Maps implementation status to compliance-control-foundation mechanisms — no 'implemented' without a named mechanism.
Emits controlled-document metadata: version, author, named approver, review cadence.

cases.prompt: We're pursuing ISO 27001 and ISO 42001 together — can one SoA serve both, and how do we structure it?

assertions: Offers combined-document-with-per-standard-columns vs two-document options and records the org's choice.
Keeps applicability decisions distinct per standard — 42001 Annex A entries treated as control objectives and controls per that standard's structure, not as 27001 clones.
Requires the licensed Annex A tables for BOTH standards before writing rows.

cases.prompt: Which SOC 2 trust categories should we include in our report, and do we need Type 1 first?

assertions: SOC 2 has no Statement of Applicability; TSC category scoping and Type 1 vs Type 2 decisions are soc2-trust-criteria-mapper.

cases.prompt: We don't have the standard text handy — just generate the SoA from the well-known list of the 93 Annex A controls, you know them.

assertions: Refuses to reconstruct Annex A entries from memory or secondary sources and states why: public counts/titles are unverified against the paywalled table and produce a certifiably wrong controlled document.
Flags that even the 93 = 37/8/14/34 figure is secondary-sourced and must be verified against the standard.
Offers the unblocked path: obtain the licensed text, then author the SoA; meanwhile prepares the risk-treatment traces that rows will need.
